seo.title: AI Agent Authentication and Authorization in Amazon Bedrock Agents Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent authentication and authorization in Amazon Bedrock Agents agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Amazon Bedrock Agents, AI Agent Authentication and Authorization issues manifest when action groups attempt unauthorized API calls via Lambda functions or when knowledge base retrievals expose sensitive data without proper IAM role scoping. Misconfigured agent IAM roles or guardrails fail to enforce domain-specific access policies, leading to privilege escalation or data leaks during multi-turn conversations. These problems are exacerbated by session state carrying over unauthorized tokens between invocations.

howItManifests.scenario: Action group Lambda lacks proper IAM permissions check
howItManifests.symptom: Lambda returns 403 Forbidden when agent invokes business logic API
howItManifests.impact: Agent conversation stalls with cryptic 'Action failed' errors

howItManifests.scenario: Knowledge base RAG retrieves sensitive documents without auth context
howItManifests.symptom: Agent returns PII/financial data to unauthorized users
howItManifests.impact: Compliance violations and potential data breach exposure

howItManifests.scenario: Guardrails not configured for domain-specific auth policies
howItManifests.symptom: Agent authorizes actions beyond user’s organizational role
howItManifests.impact: Privilege escalation allowing cross-tenant data access

howItManifests.scenario: Session state persists unauthorized tokens across turns
howItManifests.symptom: User A sees User B's conversation context and permissions
howItManifests.impact: Session hijacking and unauthorized action execution

detection.description: TypeScript detector for session auth context mismatches across turns

fixes.name: IAM Condition Keys for Action Groups
fixes.description: Restrict Lambda execution with tenant-aware IAM policies

fixes.name: Knowledge Base Metadata Filtering
fixes.description: Add tenant metadata filtering to KB vector search

fixes.name: Guardrails Domain Policy Enforcement
fixes.description: Configure Bedrock Guardrails for auth-specific content policies

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Authentication and Authorization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides continuous monitoring of Bedrock Agent invocations by wrapping invoke_agent() calls with vex.verify() post-processing that automatically detects authentication failures, cross-tenant knowledge base access, and session state drift. The platform analyzes agent trace events in real-time, correlating Lambda 403 errors with session attributes and flagging guardrail bypasses using ML-powered anomaly detection. 

Remediation workflows automatically rotate compromised IAM roles, update knowledge base metadata filters, and inject tenant context into session state. Vex maintains audit trails for compliance reporting and provides one-click fixes through its policy-as-code interface. Integration takes <5 minutes with the vex-sdk.

The verification suite runs serverlessly, analyzing 100% of agent traffic without performance impact while providing SLO/SLA monitoring for auth-related failures.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex
import boto3

bedrock = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')

@vex.verify(
  name="bedrock-agent-auth-check",
  failure_policy="BLOCK_AND_ALERT",
  detectors=[
    "lambda-403-auth-failure",
    "cross-tenant-kb-access",
    "session-auth-drift"
  ]
)
def secure_agent_invoke(agent_id: str, session_id: str, user_tenant: str, input_text: str):
    response = bedrock.invoke_agent(
        agentId=agent_id,
        agentAliasId='latest',
        sessionId=session_id,
        inputText=input_text,
        sessionState={
            'sessionAttributes': {'tenantId': user_tenant}
        }
    )
    
    # Vex auto-analyzes trace for auth violations
    verified_response = vex.enhance(response)
    return verified_response

# Usage
result = secure_agent_invoke(
    'YOUR_AGENT_ID', 
    'session-123', 
    'tenant:acme-corp',
    'Process my order #12345'
)

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install vex-sdk and wrap your Bedrock agent calls to eliminate auth failures in under 5 minutes
